name: reasoning-analogical
description: Transfer knowledge from source domains to novel target situations through structural mapping. Use when facing new markets, products, or situations where past experience provides relevant patterns. Produces adapted solutions with explicit mappings and context adjustments.
capabilities: Identify relevant prior experience with documented outcomes using weighted criteria
Extract transferable structure from source domains including objects, relations, and constraints
Map source structure to new context by identifying preserved, modified, and broken elements
Generate adapted solutions with concrete action plans and confidence scores
author: BellaBe
securitySummary: This is a pure documentation skill containing only YAML frontmatter and markdown. No executable code, scripts, network calls, filesystem access, or environment variable reads exist. The static scanner flagged documentation patterns (backticks for markdown code blocks, 'hash' in metadata field names, 'query' in YAML examples) as false positives. All 41 findings are dismissed as non-security issues in documentation context.
valueStatement: When facing new markets, products, or situations, you need a systematic way to transfer relevant knowledge from past experience. This skill provides a four-stage framework for analogical reasoning that identifies transferable patterns while accounting for context differences.
userTitle: Apply Past Experience to New Situations
limitations: Requires user to provide source cases with documented outcomes
Does not access external databases or knowledge sources
Cannot verify if mapped solutions will succeed in practice
Does not automate implementation of adapted solutions

useCases.title: Expand to New Verticals
useCases.description: Apply successful product strategies from existing verticals to new market segments with appropriate adaptations.

useCases.title: Transfer Best Practices
useCases.description: Map proven approaches from one industry to another client engagement while identifying necessary modifications.

useCases.title: Adapt Playbooks
useCases.description: Leverage experience from previous ventures or comparable companies when entering unfamiliar markets or launching new offerings.

promptTemplates.title: Basic Pattern Match
promptTemplates.prompt: We are entering [new market/domain]. We previously succeeded with [past case] in [source domain]. Help me apply analogical reasoning to map our approach.

promptTemplates.title: Product Expansion
promptTemplates.prompt: We want to build [new product type] for [new user segment]. We have experience building [existing product] for [existing segment]. Use analogical reasoning to identify what transfers and what needs adaptation.

promptTemplates.title: Cross-Domain Transfer
promptTemplates.prompt: We are facing [novel situation]. Use the four-stage analogical reasoning process: (1) Retrieve relevant source cases from [domain] with documented outcomes, (2) Extract structural elements including objects, relations, and constraints, (3) Map these to the target context identifying preserved, modified, and broken elements, (4) Generate an adapted solution with specific actions and confidence scores.

promptTemplates.title: Validation Mapping
promptTemplates.prompt: Review our planned approach for [new situation] against [successful reference case]. Identify gaps in our mapping where we may be over-transferring or under-adapting. Provide specific recommendations with confidence levels.

bestPractices: Select source cases with documented, measurable outcomes rather than anecdotal experience
Explicitly identify which elements transfer, adapt, or break for each mapping component
Assign confidence scores and document key uncertainties for each adaptation
antiPatterns: Mapping based on surface similarities rather than underlying structural relations
Assuming complete transfer without explicit context difference analysis
Using single source case without comparing multiple candidate analogies

faq.question: Which AI tools support this skill?
faq.answer: This skill is compatible with Claude, Codex, and Claude Code. It provides a reasoning framework any LLM can follow.

faq.question: How long does the analogical process take?
faq.answer: Simple mappings complete in 5-10 minutes. Complex cross-domain transfers with multiple candidate sources may take 20-30 minutes.

faq.question: How do I integrate this with other reasoning skills?
faq.answer: Use Abductive reasoning first to explain the situation, then Analogical to find relevant patterns, followed by Causal for execution planning.

faq.question: What data does this skill access?
faq.answer: This is a prompt-based skill that processes your input. It does not access external databases, files, or user data.

faq.question: Why is my mapping confidence low?
faq.answer: Low confidence typically indicates high context differences or insufficient source documentation. Retrieve additional candidate cases or accept longer validation cycles.

faq.question: How is this different from standard analogies?
faq.answer: This skill provides a systematic four-stage process with explicit mappings, quality gates, and confidence scoring rather than informal comparisons.
